source.title: Responding to Follow-Up Questions with Grace
source.summary: When people ask about your testimony: (1) 'I don't know' is a valid answer—don't make up theology on the spot; (2) 'Can I think about that and get back to you?' shows humility; (3) Redirect sincere questions to Scripture or trusted resources; (4) Avoid debating—if it becomes argumentative, return to your story ('I just know what happened to me'); (5) Don't feel obligated to answer personal questions that cross your boundaries; (6) If they challenge your experience, don't defend—let your life be the proof over time; (7) Watch for genuine curiosity vs. attempts to trap or mock you. Your testimony isn't on trial. You're sharing an invitation to encounter Jesus, not defending a dissertation.
